(1/n) Evolutionary frameworks like AlphaEvolve and GEPA use diversity and fitness to select which subset of past experiments to condition the next generation on. Why not let an agent choose instead? To this end, we introduce Coding Agents as Text Optimizers (CATO). We beat

in closing 

1) the tokens going into your harness (skill.md, claude.md, hook prompts) are SUPER HIGH LEVERAGE - they are worth painstaking over and writing by hand, or iterating on a lot w/ a model

2) the best harness engineers are minimalists, start w/ defaults, work on real

harness engineering, coined by @Vtrivedy10 is about the things you build around the model to create a good agent - as @mitchellh puts it 

> [it] is the idea that anytime you find an agent makes a mistake, you take the time to engineer a solution such that the agent never makes https://t.co/Qt3oLKSZhh

If You’re a Developer, Master These 15 Things

1. Data Structures & Algorithms
Arrays, HashMaps, Trees, Graphs, Recursion, Big-O.

2. Git & Version Control
Branching, pull requests, rebasing, resolving conflicts.

3. Debugging Skills
Reading logs > guessing fixes.

4. Clean Code

OpenCode’s rules for fighting slop:

- don’t ship features just because you can
- leave the code better than you found it
- fixing features & process > new features

Most teams can build AI agents.
Very few can operate them continuously.

Bottlenecks I keep seeing: context routing, failure recovery, cost guardrails, clean handoffs.

Lysium is an app I've been building specifically for background agent orchestration.

Features:
→ Mobile-first + cross-platform
→ Run multiple agents in parallel across repositories
→ Launch agent-based requests from issues and PRs
→ Swipe actions for close, merge, create PR, https://t.co/LWUyrVdONW

Since open-sourcing Mission Control, one thing became obvious:
people don’t struggle with “running agents” — they struggle with operating them.

The hard parts are:

• visibility across agent runs
• failure recovery / reconnect behavior
• cost + token guardrails
• task flow

VERCEL ACABA DE ROMPER EL INTERNET 🔥

Los agentes de IA ya no solo navegan por el navegador…

AHORA PUEDEN ABRIR Y CONTROLAR Slack, Discord, Notion, Figma, VS Code y Spotify como si fueran humanos de verdad.

- Sin APIs. 
- Sin OAuth. 
- Sin complicaciones.

I create/invented a new SKILL for leading Claude Code through a structured Hegelian dialectic like process for doing deep investigation of topics. I've spent 40+ hours using it over the past 10 days — super addicting.

It's as transformative for me as the original Deep Research. https://t.co/IplZXEmmKe
